blocks:
Ayah

قُلْ هُوَ ٱللَّهُ أَحَدٌ (۱)
Say, ‘He is Allah, the One. (1)

Key Points

The word "Allah" (اللّه) derives from walih (وَلِهَ), meaning bewilderment or astonishment. This means that creatures are incapable of grasping His reality and are bewildered and astonished in recognizing His Essence. Just as the Commander of the Faithful, Ali عليه السلام, states: The meaning of Allah is that He is a Deity in whom creatures are bewildered, because He is concealed from the perception of eyes and veiled from the reach of thoughts and intellects. (250)

There is a difference between the words Wāhid (واحد) and Ahad (اَحد). Wāhid is used in a context where a second or third is conceivable, contrary to Ahad, which is used for the unique and singular One. For example, if you say: "One person is not his match," it implies that several people might be his match. But if you say: "No Ahad (one) is his match," it means he has no match whatsoever.

Tawhid (Monotheism) is the boundary between faith and disbelief, and entry into the fortress of faith is impossible without acknowledging Tawhid. The first statement of the Prophet was the word of Tawhid: «قولوا لا اله الا اللّه تفلحوا» (Say, 'There is no god but Allah,' and you shall succeed).

The monotheistic slogan «لا اله الا اللّه» is composed of three letters (Alif, Lām, and Hā) and is a remembrance (dhikr) that does not even require the lips to move when spoken, but its practical application continues until the obedience of the infallible leader (Imam) and their successors; because the condition of Tawhid, according to the statement of Imam Reza عليه السلام, is faith in the infallible Imam and obedience to Him.

During the journey when Imam Reza عليه السلام was forced by Ma’mun to migrate from Medina to Marw, when He reached Nishapur along the way, people gathered around Him and asked Him for a gift and guidance. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: My father narrated from his father, and he from his ancestors, and they from the Messenger of Allah, that God has said: «كلمة لا اله الا اللّه حصنى فمن دخل حصنى أمن من عذابى» (The statement 'There is no god but Allah' is My fortress, and whoever enters My fortress shall be safe from My punishment). Then Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Of course, Tawhid has conditions, and I (who am the present Imam) am one of its conditions. That is, Tawhid without Wilayah (guardianship/leadership) is not accepted.

During the Occultation of the infallible Imam, based on their own command, we must turn to the just jurists (Fuqahā) who are far removed from whims and desires. Therefore, complete Tawhid is faith in the Oneness of Allah, keeping thought and action away from all forms of polytheism (shirk), and following the infallible leaders during their presence and the just jurists during the Occultation.

Imam Ali عليه السلام writes in a letter to his son, Imam Hassan عليه السلام: «لَوْ كانَ لِرَبِّكَ شَریكٌ لَأَتَتْكَ رُسُلَه» (If your Lord had a partner, His messengers would have come to you) so that people might recognize Him and worship Him. (251)

The proof of His Oneness is the harmony among creation. Just as Imam Sadiq عليه السلام considers the "continuity of administration" (ittiṣāl al-tadbīr) a sign of the Creator's Oneness. (252) The harmony among the sun, the moon, the earth, water, wind, soil, mountains, plains, and seas with one another, and the harmony of all of them with human needs, is a sign of the unified administration in the system of existence.

Humans take in oxygen and release carbon, while plants take in carbon and release oxygen. The needs of a child are met by the affection of parents, and the fatigue of the day is relieved by the sleep of the night. The eye, which is made of fat, is preserved by salty water, and the mouth, which is the entry point for food to the stomach, is moistened by fresh water, which is effective in digesting food.

During the Battle of the Camel, a Bedouin Arab asked Imam Ali عليه السلام about the meaning of Tawhid. The other fighters were upset with him, thinking it was an inappropriate question, but the Imam said: Our war is for the sake of Tawhid, and then He explained the correct meaning of Tawhid to him. (253)

Allah is unique not only in Essence but also in Attributes. He is not like us, whose attributes are separate from our essence. We possess knowledge and power, but we did not have them in childhood and we lose them in old age. Allah possesses knowledge and power, but these attributes did not arise for Him; rather, they have always been inherent in His Essence, just as the attribute of being created, being helpless, and being poor has never been, and never will be, separate from us.

250) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 3, p. 222. 251) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 31. 252) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 3, p. 418. 253) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Prophets are trustees of revelation; God told the Prophet: «قل...» (Say...), and He also says: «قل...» (Say...).

2- Allah, despite His presence, is absent from sight and cannot be observed. «هو» (The people of Tawhid believe that «لا تدركه الابصار و هو یدرك الابصار» (254) Eyes do not perceive Him, but He perceives eyes.)

3- Theological questions must be answered. «قل هو اللّه...»

4- True beliefs must be declared to others. «قل هو اللّه...»

5- Allah is unique in everything: in Essence and Attributes, in knowledge, power, life, and wisdom, in creation and granting existence. «قل هو اللّه احد»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Tawhid Some People of the Book and polytheists asked the Prophet to introduce his God to them. This Surah, which serves as the identity card of Allah, was revealed, declaring Allah pure and transcendent from having a father, mother, child, spouse, likeness, or partner.

This Surah contains the purest monotheistic beliefs, hence it is named Surah Tawhid (Monotheism) and Surah Ikhlas (Sincerity).

Many narrations have been reported regarding the virtue of this Surah, some of which are mentioned here: (248) Recite this Surah at least once in your prayers every day and night, and after it, say: «كذلك اللّه ربّى» (My Lord is such).

According to narrations, this Surah is equivalent in importance to one-third of the Quran. Reciting it once is equal to reciting one-third of the Quran, reciting it twice is equivalent to two-thirds, and reciting it three times is equal to reciting the entire Quran.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Surah Tawhid is like Ali ibn Abi Talib, in that whoever loves him sincerely in their heart possesses one-third of faith; whoever loves him with heart and tongue possesses two-thirds of faith; and whoever is with him through heart, tongue, and obedience in action, their faith is complete.

The recitation of this Surah is considered one of the post-prayer supplications (ta’qībāt), and we read in narrations: Whoever does not abandon its recitation after every prayer, Allah grants him the good of this world and the Hereafter, and forgives him, his parents, and his children.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: Because Allah knew that a precise and profound group would come in the latter days, He revealed Surah Tawhid and the first six verses of Surah Al-Hadid.

Reciting this Surah towards the six directions causes a person to be protected from dangers and secured from calamities. (249)

248) The collection of narrations is found in Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, (Vol. 5, pp. 699 - 715). 249) Misbah al-Kaf’ami, p. 246.

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلصَّمَدُ (۲)
Allah is the All-embracing. (2)

Key Points

"Al-Ṣamad" (الصمد) means to intend or aim for. Allah is Al-Ṣamad, meaning all beings intend for Him in their affairs, and He is the object of intention for everyone. As Imam Jawad عليه السلام said regarding the meaning of "Al-Ṣamad": «سیّدٌ مصمودٌ الیه:» "He is a Master whom beings turn to and are in need of." This is similar to the Ayah: «انّ الى ربّك المنتهى».(255) Imam Husayn عليه السلام, in response to the people of Basra who had asked about the meaning of Al-Ṣamad, said: The meaning of Al-Ṣamad is the same as the phrases that follow it, namely «لم یلد و لم یولد».(256) Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said in the interpretation of Al-Ṣamad: «هو اللّه الصمد الّذى لا من شى ء و لا فى شى ء و لا على شى ء، مبدع الأشیاء و خالقها و منشئ الأشیاء بقدرته، یتلاشى ما خلق للفناء بمشیّته و یبقى ما خلق للبقاء بعلمه»(257) Allah is Al-Ṣamad, meaning He did not originate from anything, He is not contained within anything, and He is not resting upon anything. He is the Originator of things and their Creator, and the Producer of things by His Power. What He created for annihilation disintegrates by His Will, and what He created for permanence remains by His Knowledge. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: If I found those worthy of it, I would extract Tawhid (monotheism), Islam, and Iman (faith) from this very word, "Al-Ṣamad."(258)

256) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 257) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 258) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The attention of all beings, whether willingly or unwillingly, is directed towards Him. «اللّه الصمد»

2- Only He is Self-Sufficient, and everyone is in need of Him. «اللّه الصمد»

3- Only Allah is worthy of being intended. «اللّه الصمد» (The Alif and Lam in Al-Ṣamad are for exclusivity/restriction.)

4- Beings are in need of Him in all their affairs. «اللّه الصمد» (The word Ṣamad is absolute and includes all the material and spiritual needs of the creation.)

Ayah

لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ (۳)
He neither begat, nor was begotten, (3)

Key Points

The relationship between Allah and creation is a relationship of creation, not procreation (or begetting). He creates beings, meaning He brings them from non-existence into existence, rather than begetting them Himself. This is because a newborn is of the same nature as the parents and is, in fact, a part of them, whereas nothing is of the same nature as Allah or a part of Him.

Since most polytheists and People of the Book attributed a son to Allah, therefore, «لم یلد» (He begets not), which means not having a child, precedes «لم یولد» (nor is He begotten).(259)

Beings are not only unbegotten by Him, but they do not even issue forth from Him, in the way that fruit issues from the flower, the tree from the seed, water from the cloud and spring, fire from wood, speech from the mouth, writing from the pen, scent from the flower, taste from food, thought from the intellect, heat from fire, cold from ice, sleep and imagination from the mind, and sorrow, joy, fear, and hope from the heart.(260)

This Ayah refutes the belief of Christianity, who consider Jesus the son of God, the belief of the Jews, who considered Uzayr the son of God, and the belief of the polytheists of the Age of Ignorance, who imagined the angels to be the daughters of God. 259) Tafsir Novin.

260) Part of the above content originates from the statement of Imam Husayn عليه السلام in response to the people of Basra who had asked him about the meaning of Samad. (Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn)

Messages

1- Allah's Samadiyyah (Absolute Independence) and His needlessness of everything prove that He has no need for children or parents. «اللّه الصمد. لم یلد و لم یولد»

2- Allah has no cause and did not originate from anything. «و لم یولد»

3- That which is begotten (mawlūd) is an effect (ma‘lūl) and cannot be God. «و لم یولد» (How can Christians consider Jesus the begotten son of Mary, yet still regard him as God?)

Ayah

وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ كُفُوًا أَحَدٌۢ (٤)
nor has He any equal.’ (4)

Key Points

Kufw (كُفْو) means similar, equal, or like. (261) Just as in discussions about marriage, it is recommended that you choose a spouse who is kufw (compatible) with you. Meaning, they should be similar and like you in religion and morals. Allah is Aṣ-Ṣamad (the Eternal Refuge), meaning He is independent of others, and everyone is dependent on Him. Therefore, He cannot have an equal, because His equal would have to be independent of Him, which is incompatible with Allah being Aṣ-Ṣamad. The previous Ayah negated the possibility of Allah being a parent or having a child; this Ayah negates the existence of any spouse for Him. Just as He says in another Ayah: «ما اتّخذ صاحبةً و لا ولداً» (262) He has neither taken a spouse nor has He a son.

261) Nihāyah Ibn Athīr. 262) Al-Jinn, 3.

Messages

1- Neither in His Essence, nor in His Attributes, nor in His Actions, is anyone or anything similar or like Allah. «و لم یكن له كفوا احد»

2- Allah has no equal who could become His partner in the affairs of existence. «و لم یكن له كفوا احد» «والحمد للّه ربّ العالمین»
